text_blocks.text: The other major method of producing 0 2
gas involves passing a stream of clean dry air
through one bed of a pair of identical zeolite
molecular sieves, which absorbs the nitrogen and
delivers a gas stream that is 90% to 93% 0 2.
Simultaneously, nitrogen gas is released from the
other nitrogen-saturated zeolite bed, by reducing the
chamber operating pressure and diverting part of
the oxygen gas from the producer bed through it,
in the reverse direction of flow. After a set cycle
time the operation of the two beds is interchanged,
thereby allowing for a continuous supply of gaseous
oxygen to be pumped through a pipeline. This is
known as pressure swing adsorption. Oxygen gas is
technologies (see also the related vacuum swing
